zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywel於1274年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根利安善納時，她的父親Aron ap Hywel Fychan24歲，母親Dyddgu verch Adam Fychan22歲。她和Rhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwy至少有2個女兒。她去世於1339年在英國威爾斯丹比爾伯頓享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Aron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Aaron
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the brother of Moses, who was appointed by God to be Moses' spokesman and became the first High Priest of the Israelites (Exodus 4:14–16, 7:1–2). It is of uncertain origin and meaning: most probably, like Moses , of Egyptian rather than Hebrew origin. The traditional derivation from Hebrew har-on ‘mountain of strength’ is no more than a folk etymology. The name has been in regular use from time immemorial as a Jewish name and was taken up by the Nonconformists as a Christian name in the 16th century. Since the late 1990s it has been widely popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywel was born in 1274, in Llansannor, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Aron ap Hywel Fychan, was 24 and her mother, Dyddgu verch Adam Fychan, was 22. She had at least 2 daughters with Rhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwy. She died in 1339, in Burton, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywelが1274年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンランサノーで生まれた時，父Aron ap Hywel Fychanは24歳，母Dyddgu verch Adam Fychanは22歳であった。彼女はRhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwyとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1339年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアブルトンで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywel(이)가 1274년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 랜산어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Aron ap Hywel Fychan(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dyddgu verch Adam Fychan(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Rhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwy(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1339년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 버턴에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywel родилась в 1274 г. в местности ллансаннор, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Aron ap Hywel Fychan было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Dyddgu verch Adam Fychan, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Rhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwy. Она умерла в 1339 г. в местности буртон, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Verch Aron Ap Hywel nació en 1274, en Llansannor, Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Aron ap Hywel Fychan, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Dyddgu verch Adam Fychan, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Rhûn "Gronwy" ap Gronwy. Murió en 1339, en Burton, Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años.
